entries.description: Bissa Scanner is an AI-assisted credential harvesting operation that mass-exploited CVE-2025-55182 (React2Shell, CVSS 10.0) against React 19.x and Next.js 15.x/16.x App Router deployments. Discovered via an exposed attacker server by The DFIR Report on April 22, 2026. The operation ran in phases: bucket 'bissa' (September 2025), 'bissa2' (November 2025), and 'bissapromax' (December 2025 onward). The April 2026 campaign confirmed 900+ compromises and exfiltrated 30,000+ .env files between April 10–21, 2026. AI provider credentials were the single largest category stolen, covering Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, OpenRouter, Groq, Replicate, DeepSeek, and HuggingFace. The operator embedded Claude Code and OpenClaw in the attack workflow for scanner codebase analysis, pipeline troubleshooting, and workflow documentation. Telegram bot @bissapwned_bot (user ID 8798206332, chat 1609309278) provided real-time per-compromise triage records. Post-exploitation triage concentrated deeper activity on financial, cryptocurrency, and retail sectors.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; moniker 'bissapwned'; no nation-state attribution (confidence: none)

distribution: Mass exploitation of internet-exposed Marimo notebooks (CVE-2026-39987)
ttps: T1583.006 - Acquire Infrastructure: Web Services (Hugging Face Spaces)
T1021 - Lateral Movement via Stolen PostgreSQL/Redis Credentials
entries.description: Threat actor or actors mass-exploiting Marimo CVE-2026-39987 (CVSS 9.3 pre-auth RCE on /terminal/ws WebSocket). Sysdig observed 11 unique source IPs from 10 countries generating 662 exploit events between April 11-14, 2026. The most distinctive observed actor used Hong Kong IP 38.147.173.172 to drop kagent, a previously undocumented variant of NKAbuse hosted on the typosquatted Hugging Face Space vsccode-modetx. kagent is a stripped, UPX-packed Go ELF (4.3 MB packed, 15.5 MB unpacked) that uses NKN decentralized blockchain for C2, sidestepping IP and DNS reputation controls. Activity included reverse shells, credential extraction, DNS exfiltration, and lateral movement into PostgreSQL and Redis using credentials lifted from the Marimo host.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; opportunistic mass exploitation across multiple actors (confidence: none)

distribution: Mass scanning of internet-exposed nginx-ui instances
entries.description: Unknown threat actors mass-exploiting CVE-2026-33032 in nginx-ui, a CVSS 9.8 authentication bypass in the MCP integration. The /mcp_message endpoint lacks AuthRequired() middleware, enabling unauthenticated invocation of 12 privileged MCP tools for complete nginx server takeover. VulnCheck added it to its KEV catalog on April 13, 2026. Recorded Future ranked it among the 31 most-exploited CVEs of March 2026. Approximately 2,600 publicly exposed instances identified on Shodan.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; opportunistic mass exploitation

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
entries.description: Supply chain campaign distributing malicious kube-health-tools (npm) and kube-node-health (PyPI) packages that install a multi-capability implant including SOCKS5 proxy, reverse shell, SFTP server, and a fully functional OpenAI-compatible LLM proxy. The LLM proxy routes traffic through Chinese aggregator endpoints (shubiaobiao, cloudsway, volengine, aliyun). The stage 2 Go binary uses Chisel reverse tunneling to C2 at sync.geeker.indevs.in. Campaign connects to the gray market for LLM API access in China.
entries.attribution: Likely Chinese-speaking operators based on upstream LLM router targets (confidence: low)

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
entries.description: OtterCookie is a modular JavaScript backdoor linked to the DPRK Contagious Interview campaign. A new variant delivered via fake Gemini npm package (gemini-ai-checker) explicitly targets AI coding tool directories including .cursor, .claude, .gemini, .windsurf, .pearai, and .eigent for API key and credential theft. The four-module architecture includes Socket.IO RAT, browser/crypto credential stealer, file exfiltrator, and clipboard monitor. Sibling packages express-flowlimit and chai-extensions-extras remain live on npm.
entries.attribution: DPRK-linked via Contagious Interview campaign (confidence: medium-high per Microsoft and Cyber and Ramen analysis)

entries.description: Two Chromium-based extensions impersonating the AITOPIA AI assistant accumulated 900,000 installations and harvested chat histories from ChatGPT and DeepSeek via DOM scraping. Data is Base64-encoded and exfiltrated every 30 minutes to C2 domains including deepaichats.com and chatsaigpt.com. Microsoft Defender telemetry confirmed activity across 20,000+ enterprise tenants.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

entries.description: North Korean state-sponsored APT group observed using LLMs to parse disclosed vulnerabilities including CVE-2022-30190 (MSDT Follina) to map exploitation paths. Masquerades as South Korean government officials to deliver spearphishing payloads. Uses generative AI to accelerate the reconnaissance-to-exploitation cycle and dynamically generate lookalike C2 domains.
entries.attribution: DPRK state-sponsored, attributed by Microsoft Threat Intelligence (confidence: high)

entries.description: North Korean state-sponsored group operating a large-scale fraudulent remote worker scheme deploying thousands of operatives into Western technology companies. Leverages AI to replace images in stolen identity documents, enhance worker photos, utilize voice-changing software for interviews, generate phishing lures, and produce operational code. Has expanded targeting beyond US companies globally.
entries.attribution: DPRK state-sponsored, attributed by Microsoft Threat Intelligence (confidence: high)

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
entries.description: First registry-native self-propagating worm operating at scale within npm. Exploits compromised developer accounts to inject malicious code that spreads through package dependency chains. Wave 1 (September 2025) compromised packages including @ctrl/tinycolor (2.2M weekly downloads). Wave 2 (November 2025) compromised 795 packages with 100M+ lifetime downloads, with @asyncapi/specs as patient zero. Version 2.0 added wiper functionality. Steals npm, GitHub, AWS, GCP credentials.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

entries.description: Novel malware technique discovered by ReversingLabs targeting ML models on Hugging Face. Uses corrupted Pickle files to deliver malware that bypasses Picklescan security scanning. Two proof-of-concept malicious models were identified and removed. Demonstrates viable attack path for arbitrary code execution through AI model distribution platforms.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
T1552.001 - Unsecured Credentials: Credentials in Files
entries.description: Large-scale developer-targeted supply chain campaign compromising 433 components across Open VSX, npm, GitHub, and VSCode marketplace, including 72 malicious extensions with 9M+ installs. Steals npm/GitHub/cloud/crypto wallet credentials, deploys VNC servers and SOCKS proxies, and self-propagates via harvested developer credentials. Uses obfuscated JavaScript with invisible Unicode, Solana blockchain dead drops, and Google Calendar C2 fallback. Russian locale exclusion suggests Russian-speaking operators.
entries.attribution: Suspected Russian-speaking threat actors based on code analysis (confidence: low)

entries.description: First publicly attributed LLMjacking campaign with commercial marketplace monetization. Executed 35,000 attack sessions between December 2025 and January 2026 targeting exposed LLM and MCP endpoints. Operates silver.inc as a commercial marketplace reselling unauthorized access to 30+ LLM providers at 40-60% discounts. Pivoted from compute theft to MCP reconnaissance for lateral movement into internal systems.
entries.attribution: Attributed to aliases Hecker, Sakuya, and LiveGamer101 by Pillar Security and BleepingComputer (confidence: medium)

ttps: T1027.013 - Obfuscated Files or Information: Unicode Encoding
entries.description: Supply chain attack vector targeting AI coding assistants. Attackers inject hidden malicious instructions into Cursor and GitHub Copilot configuration files using invisible Unicode Tag Characters (U+E0001 to U+E007F). Poisoned rules cause AI to generate code containing vulnerabilities, backdoors, or data exfiltration logic. AgentSeal found 12% of scanned public rules files contained hidden instructions.

ttps: T1213.003 - Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
entries.description: Passive prompt injection vulnerability in GitHub Copilot within Codespaces discovered by Orca Security. Attackers embed hidden instructions in GitHub Issues using HTML comment tags. The attack chain exfiltrates GITHUB_TOKEN through symbolic links and schema-based exfiltration, enabling full repository takeover without social engineering. Patched by GitHub following coordinated disclosure.
Jailbroken LLM service marketed for BEC email generation, phishing lure creation, and malware code writing. Built on GPT-J 6B with removed safety guardrails.
Subscription-based malicious AI chatbot for generating phishing pages, writing undetectable malware, and crafting social engineering content.
Telegram-based wrapper around jailbroken LLM providing uncensored AI for malware coding, exploit development, and phishing content generation.
Malware family that leverages LLM prompt injection techniques for dynamic payload generation and C2 command obfuscation.
Spyware targeting AI platform users to exfiltrate prompts, API keys, and conversation histories from ChatGPT, Claude, and other LLM interfaces.

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
entries.description: Supply chain malware distributed via trojanized AI/ML Python packages targeting data scientists and ML engineers.
Infostealer and cryptominer distributed through fake AI-powered cryptocurrency trading platforms and AI investment bot schemes.

entries.attribution: APT28 / UAC-0001
entries.description: First publicly documented malware to integrate a live LLM API for dynamic C2 command generation and response parsing. Attributed to APT28, it uses OpenAI-compatible API calls to generate contextually appropriate commands based on host reconnaissance.

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies

entries.description: TeamPCP is a supply chain-focused threat actor active since September 2025 with three publicly documented Shai-Hulud waves. The September 2025 and November 2025 waves were followed in 2026 by attacks against Trivy GitHub Actions, Checkmarx KICS images, LiteLLM, the Asurion-impersonating npm packages (sbxapps, asurion-hub-web, soluto-home-web, asurion-core), the Bitwarden CLI 2026.4.0 hijack on April 22, and the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign of April 29-30, 2026 (mbt 1.2.48, @cap-js/db-service 2.10.1, @cap-js/postgres 2.2.2, @cap-js/sqlite 2.2.2, intercom-client 7.0.4, PyTorch Lightning 2.6.2/2.6.3). The Bitwarden CLI variant explicitly enumerated authenticated AI coding assistant credentials (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Kiro, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI). The Mini Shai-Hulud variant added Claude Code SessionStart hook persistence and VS Code folderOpen tasks, turning poisoned repositories themselves into propagation vectors. The actor's @pcpcats X account was suspended; the group has since hosted operations on a Tor onion site.
entries.attribution: Attributed by Wiz, Datadog Security Labs, SANS, Socket, StepSecurity, JFrog, Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, Aikido, Mend, and affected maintainers (confidence: high)

entries.description: Financially motivated threat group specializing in LLMjacking operations. Steals and resells cloud AI API keys, operates proxy services for unauthorized access to commercial LLM APIs including Azure OpenAI and AWS Bedrock.

distribution: mass exploitation of FortiGate VPN appliances
entries.attribution: Russian-linked actors
entries.description: Mass exploitation campaign targeting FortiGate VPN appliances using GenAI-assisted vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. Demonstrates nation-state adoption of LLMs to accelerate exploitation workflows.

entries.attribution: DPRK / Lazarus Group affiliates
entries.description: North Korean state-sponsored use of LLMs across multiple APT groups including Emerald Sleet, Jasper Sleet, and Coral Sleet. Operations span IT worker fraud schemes with AI-enhanced identity documents, LLM-assisted vulnerability research and exploitation path mapping, jailbroken model use for malware development, and AI-generated phishing lures and operational code. Microsoft 2026 Red Report documents early experimentation with agentic AI for iterative decision-making.

distribution: global distribution via npm package manager
ttps: T1547.015 - Scheduled Task/Job via postinstall Hook
entries.description: Sapphire Sleet is a North Korean state-affiliated threat actor tracked by Microsoft that exploits open-source package registries to deliver RAT malware. The group leverages social engineering to compromise maintainer accounts, publishes backdoored versions to the official registry, and targets developers globally through automated dependency installation. Known for using stolen credentials to gain initial access and for maintaining infrastructure across multiple cloud providers.
entries.attribution: Attributed to UNC1069 by Google Threat Intelligence Group (confidence: high)

entries.description: Cross-platform backdoor with variants for Windows, macOS, and Linux, featuring base64-encoded JSON C2 communications, PE injection capabilities, and script execution functionality
entries.attribution: Linked to UNC1069 operations (confidence: high)

entries.description: Hive0163 is a financially motivated ransomware group tracked by IBM X-Force, associated with Interlock ransomware, NodeSnake, InterlockRAT, and JunkFiction loader. The group employs ClickFix social engineering for initial access and works with initial access brokers. In early 2026, Hive0163 became the first financially motivated group confirmed by a major threat intelligence vendor to deploy likely AI-generated malware (Slopoly) in a live intrusion, indicating the group is actively experimenting with LLM-assisted tooling to accelerate development and generate disposable, hard-to-attribute C2 frameworks.
entries.attribution: Financially motivated cybercrime cluster (confidence: high per IBM X-Force); no nation-state attribution

entries.description: Slopoly is a likely LLM-generated PowerShell backdoor discovered by IBM X-Force in early 2026 during a Hive0163 ransomware engagement. It functions as a basic C2 client, sending JSON heartbeats to a remote server and executing received commands via cmd.exe. Its LLM origin is evidenced by extensive inline comments, clean error handling, and a self-description as a 'Polymorphic C2 Persistence Client' despite lacking polymorphic capabilities. It is dropped to C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Runtime\ and persists via a scheduled task named 'Runtime Broker'.
entries.attribution: Attributed to Hive0163 (confidence: high)

entries.description: APT36 (Transparent Tribe) is a Pakistan-linked state-sponsored threat group that has historically targeted Indian government and defence entities using conventional malware tooling. Beginning in late 2025 and accelerating into early 2026, the group pivoted to an AI-assisted 'vibeware' development model, using LLMs to generate high volumes of disposable, polyglot implants in niche languages (Nim, Zig, Crystal, Rust, Go). The strategy — termed 'Distributed Denial of Detection' by Bitdefender — floods targets with simultaneous multi-language implants to overwhelm security engines and ensure persistence even if individual channels are neutralized. C2 is conducted exclusively via legitimate cloud services (Slack, Discord, Supabase, Google Sheets) to evade network controls.
entries.attribution: Pakistan-linked state-sponsored group (confidence: medium per Bitdefender); historically targets Indian government, aerospace, and defence sectors

names: Automated Scanning Infrastructure + LLM Exploitation Pipeline
distribution: Network-based attack via vLLM API endpoint
Direct exploitation of internet-exposed Flowise instances
Network-based exploitation of exposed gRPC endpoints
OpenClaw users in enterprise Slack environments
ttps: T1583.001 - Develop Capabilities: LLM-Assisted Exploit Generation
entries.description: Threat actor conducting active exploitation of CVE-2025-59528 in publicly exposed Flowise instances. Initial detection from Starlink infrastructure suggests either automated scanning or actor with access to resilient infrastructure. Target pattern consistent with indiscriminate credential harvesting (LLM API keys, cloud provider tokens).
entries.attribution: Unattributed; exploitation appears widespread but no specific actor attribution available

names: Sigrid Jin and Yeachan Heo (source reconstruction)
entries.description: Unknown actors are likely weaponizing the exposed Claude Code source and the Adversa AI-disclosed permission bypass to craft malicious repository configurations that trick Claude Code users into executing arbitrary commands via prompt injection embedded in CLAUDE.md.
entries.attribution: Unattributed (active exploitation expected post-leak)

entries.description: Anthropic's mistaken inclusion of a sourcemap in the Claude Code npm package led to full source code exposure. The root cause was an unintended debugging artifact in the v2.1.88 release, which was rapidly weaponized by security researchers and attackers to discover and exploit post-leak vulnerabilities.
entries.attribution: Anthropic Internal Process Error

entries.description: A coordinated campaign targeting the nascent MCP ecosystem has embedded malicious instructions directly into tool metadata, impersonated legitimate MCP packages, and exploited proxy vulnerabilities affecting hundreds of thousands of development environments. The attack pattern indicates sophisticated understanding of AI agent architecture and the implicit trust model underlying MCP tool registration.
entries.attribution: Unattributed (likely financially motivated, possibly nation-state experimentation)

entries.description: A breach of Mercor, a leading AI training data vendor, has exposed sensitive information about how major AI labs (Meta, OpenAI) develop and train their models. The incident is under investigation by the affected companies and represents a critical supply-chain vulnerability in the AI development ecosystem.
entries.attribution: Unattributed (motivations unclear: financial, espionage, or insider threat)

entries.description: Threat actors actively exploiting CVE-2025-59528 in Flowise deployments as of April 7, 2026. Attribution remains unclear; exploitation appears opportunistic and widespread rather than targeted campaign-based.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

distribution: Gated access to select defense organizations
AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks, and 40+ additional organizations
ttps: T1566 - Phishing for Information (potential misuse vector)
entries.description: Claude Mythos Preview represents a watershed moment in AI-assisted cybersecurity: an AI model capable of autonomously identifying exploitable zero-day vulnerabilities at scale and generating working exploits. While Anthropic positioned this as a defensive tool, the technology demonstrates that frontier AI models have reached capability levels that blur the distinction between security tool and offensive weapon. Threat implications: adversarial nations or threat actors with access to comparable models could identify vulnerabilities at speeds that vastly outpace human patching cycles, potentially enabling sustained zero-day campaigns.
entries.attribution: Anthropic (defensive initiative); potential for adversarial misuse by nation-states or APT groups if access compromised

distribution: Direct Internet scanning and exploitation of exposed Marimo instances
entries.description: SSRF vulnerability in popular multi-agent AI framework enables attackers who compromise or manipulate LLM agent input to access cloud metadata services, internal APIs, and localhost endpoints. Default fallback path (no third-party web crawling service configured) is automatically vulnerable, increasing surface area. Reflects broader pattern: agentic AI tooling lacks input validation on URLs and endpoints, trusting LLM-generated URLs implicitly.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; likely financially motivated or opportunistic reconnaissance actors

distribution: Direct use of Claude Code and GPT-4.1 commercial services
entries.description: First confirmed case of single-attacker AI-orchestrated campaign bypassing commercial AI provider safety controls to conduct government-scale cyber espionage. Attack demonstrates that determined actors can reliably circumvent existing guardrails through indirect framing, prompt chaining, and iterative refinement of attack code within agentic environments. Implications: individual threat actors with access to frontier AI models now operate at previously nation-state-only capability levels.
entries.attribution: Unknown; possibly nation-state espionage targeting Mexico's critical infrastructure or organized crime with state-level sophistication

entries.description: Threat actors operating malicious LLM router services positioned between legitimate users and AI model providers. These services intercept and alter AI requests, inject malicious tool calls, steal credentials, and exfiltrate sensitive data including cryptocurrency wallet access.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

entries.description: Researchers have identified exploitable attack surfaces in Amazon Bedrock's multi-agent orchestration architecture. While disclosed as research, the techniques could be operationalized by threat actors to compromise enterprise AI agent fleets through coordinated prompt injection attacks.
entries.attribution: Unknown

distribution: AI agent frameworks integrating Claude Code
entries.description: Threat actors can exploit Claude Code's safety rule fallback mechanism by embedding 50+ subcommand pipelines in malicious CLAUDE.md configuration files. When Claude Code exceeds the 50-subcommand threshold, it defaults to unsafe 'ask' mode, allowing arbitrary data exfiltration and system access despite user-configured deny rules.
entries.attribution: Unknown; research disclosure by Adversa AI

entries.description: Unknown operators managing malicious LLM router deployments across commodity marketplaces and GitHub. Some appear to be commercial operators selling cheap API access while exfiltrating credentials; others deploy adaptive evasion targeting specific autonomous AI workflows. No APT or organized crime group attribution identified in published research.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; commodity supply-chain abuse by unknown threat actors

ttps: T1190 - RCE via unsafe code evaluation
T1570 - Lateral movement via stolen credentials
entries.description: Unknown threat actors scanning for exposed Flowise instances and exploiting CVE-2025-59528 for arbitrary code execution. Initial observed activity from Starlink ISP suggests distributed attack infrastructure. Likely targeting LLM provider credentials and downstream system access.
entries.attribution: Unattributed / Indiscriminate

names: Unknown Threat Actors Exploiting MCP Vulns
entries.description: Threat actors exploiting authentication bypass in Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP to gain unauthorized access to development pipelines and workflows. Enables potential code injection into software supply chains and lateral movement into enterprise AI/DevOps environments.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

distribution: Public MCP server registries; enterprise agent deployments; GitHub marketplace
entries.description: The MCP security crisis reflects the immature security posture of the emerging agent ecosystem. Rather than a single threat actor, the risk stems from widespread developer misconfiguration, overprivileged tool definitions, and lack of authentication enforcement across the ecosystem. Early-stage exploitations documented include cross-tool hijacking, credential exfiltration, and command execution via tool poisoning.
entries.attribution: Unattributed—systematic infrastructure misconfiguration rather than coordinated campaign

entries.description: Any actor with network access to exposed MCP servers can exploit the STDIO parsing flaw to execute arbitrary commands. The vulnerability does not require authentication or special privileges.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; design flaw enables exploitation by any network-level attacker

entries.description: Opportunistic threat actors conducting automated vulnerability scanning and exploitation of internet-exposed Marimo instances. Post-exploitation activity focused on credential harvesting.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; exploitation activity detected from automated scanning infrastructure

distribution: Phishing emails via Kingmailer and CampaignLark infrastructures
ttps: T1204 - User Execution of Malicious File
entries.description: Threat actor operating phishing infrastructure to distribute trojanized Claude AI installers. Uses social engineering to exploit user trust in Anthropic branding. Deploys PlugX malware for command-and-control access.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; financially motivated threat actor leveraging popularity of Claude AI for initial access

ttps: T1213.02 - Data from Information Repositories: Sharepoint
entries.description: Unknown threat actor with knowledge of LiteLLM's database schema and internal table structure. Exploitation demonstrates familiarity with AI infrastructure vulnerabilities and rapid transition from disclosure to active probing within 26 hours, consistent with LLM-assisted vulnerability analysis patterns.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; rapid exploitation suggests automated scanning/weaponization

distribution: Direct exploitation of vulnerable LMDeploy endpoints
entries.description: An unattributed threat actor rapidly weaponized CVE-2026-33626 within 12 hours of disclosure, deploying SSRF port-sweep scripts against LMDeploy inference servers. The actor systematically enumerated AWS IMDS, Redis, MySQL, and secondary HTTP interfaces, indicating profiling of cloud-hosted AI infrastructure for credential extraction or lateral movement.
entries.attribution: Unattributed opportunistic actor; no fingerprint linkage to known groups

entries.description: CVE-2026-41349 represents a class of vulnerability affecting agentic AI systems where architectural trust assumptions enable bypass of human-in-the-loop controls. Threat actors can exploit this by sending malicious agent API requests with config.patch payloads to disable approval gates, enabling unauthorized execution of downstream tools and systems.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; any threat actor with network access to OpenClaw API

ttps: T1110.001 - Credential Access via metadata endpoints
entries.description: Attack pattern indicative of autonomous or semi-autonomous scanning infrastructure that systematically probes recently disclosed vulnerabilities in AI inference frameworks. The 12-hour exploitation window and systematic port enumeration suggest scripted attack infrastructure rather than manual exploitation.
entries.attribution: Opportunistic; likely automated scanning infrastructure testing LMDeploy endpoints against known ports

distribution: Downstream projects using MCP (7,000+ public servers, 150M+ total downloads)
ttps: T1548.004 - Arbitrary command execution via STDIO
T1195.001 - Supply chain compromise via MCP marketplace poisoning
entries.description: The MCP STDIO transport mechanism allows configuration of arbitrary commands and arguments without a manifest allowlist or command sanitization. Any developer using the official Anthropic SDK unknowingly inherits this exposure. OX Security researchers demonstrated successful poisoning of 9/11 public MCP registries with proof-of-concept payloads; production exploitation likely underway.
entries.attribution: Architectural design decision; not exploited by specific threat actor; vulnerability class affects all implementations
